title_en: Islamic Sciences Catalogued: History, Geography, and Language
title_id: Ilmu-ilmu Islam dalam Kasysyaf: Sejarah, Geografi, dan Bahasa
content_id:
Literatur sejarah yang didokumentasikan dalam Kasysyaf al-Zunun sangat kaya, mencerminkan tradisi kesadaran sejarah Islam yang kuat dan sentralitas keilmuan biografis bagi transmisi pengetahuan agama. Haji Khalifah mengatalogi kronik-kronik universal besar (ta'rikh 'amm) dari sejarah al-Thabari yang monumental tentang para nabi dan raja-raja hingga kronik-kronik Mamluk dan Utsmani besar dari eranya sendiri. Beliau juga mendokumentasikan genre sejarah-sejarah lokal dan kronik-kronik analitis yang dipelihara oleh kota-kota, dinasti-dinasti, dan komunitas-komunitas keilmuan tertentu. Tradisi kamus biografi (thabaqat) mendapat perhatian khusus: Haji Khalifah mendaftarkan karya-karya thabaqat utama untuk setiap disiplin dan mazhab keilmuan, dari Thabaqat al-Kubra karya Ibn Sa'd (yang mencakup para sahabat dan tabi'in) hingga kamus-kamus biografi yang berspesifikasi pada disiplin untuk para fuqaha, ulama hadis, qurra, ahli tata bahasa, dokter, dan penyair dari setiap era besar.

Genre kamus biografi (tarajim) adalah salah satu kontribusi paling khas dari tradisi keilmuan Islam kepada sastra dunia, dan cakupan Kasysyaf al-Zunun atasnya sangat berharga. Karya-karya seperti Wafayat al-A'yan karya Ibn Khallikan, Siyar A'lam al-Nubala' karya al-Dzahabi, dan Syajarat al-Dzahab karya Ibn al-Imad al-Hanbali mendapat entri-entri terperinci. Haji Khalifah mencatat prinsip organisasi setiap karya (kronologis, alfabetis, atau berdasarkan generasi keilmuan), kriteria pengarang untuk dimasukkan, dan periode waktu yang dicakup. Beliau juga mengatalogi kamus-kamus biografi khusus yang berfokus pada wilayah, dinasti, atau komunitas keilmuan tertentu, termasuk banyak karya biografi yang didedikasikan untuk ulama-ulama Utsmani yang telah terakumulasi pada zamannya. Karya-karya biografi regional ini sering kali merupakan sumber utama untuk silsilah keilmuan dan jalur-jalur intelektual di luar tanah-tanah Arab.

Literatur geografis yang dikatalogkan oleh Haji Khalifah membentang dari panduan-panduan rute praktis (masalik wa mamalik) dari abad ke-9 dan ke-10 hingga geografi-geografi ensiklopedis besar dari periode abad pertengahan dan hingga geografi administratif Utsmani dari zamannya sendiri. Karya-karya seperti Ahsan al-Taqasim karya al-Muqaddasi, Kitab al-Rujari karya al-Idrisi, dan Mu'jam al-Buldan karya Yaqut al-Hamawi mendapat entri-entri substansial. Genre literatur perjalanan (rihlah), yang secara khusus berkaitan dengan keilmuan Maliki di dunia Islam bagian barat, juga terwakili dengan baik. Haji Khalifah sendiri adalah seorang geograf terkemuka, dan cakupannya terhadap genre ini mencerminkan keahlian pribadi: beliau dapat menilai keandalan klaim-klaim geografis tertentu dan hubungan antara berbagai karya dalam tradisi dengan cara yang tidak dapat dilakukan oleh seorang bibliograf non-spesialis.

Ilmu-ilmu bahasa Arab, yang mencakup tata bahasa, leksikografi, retorika, dan prosodi, didokumentasikan dalam Kasysyaf al-Zunun dengan ketuntasan yang diharapkan mengingat pentingnya fundamental mereka bagi semua ilmu-ilmu Islam lainnya. Para thabaqat ahli tata bahasa dan karya-karya gramatika utama dari al-Kitab karya Sibawayhi hingga tata bahasa-tata bahasa madrasa Utsmani mendapat entri-entri yang cermat. Cakupan Haji Khalifah atas tradisi leksikografis sangat luas, mendaftarkan kamus-kamus klasik utama (al-Shihah karya al-Jawhari, Mu'jam karya Ibn Faris, al-Qamus al-Muhith karya al-Fairuzabadi beserta syarahnya Taj al-Arus) bersama kamus-kamus khusus untuk istilah-istilah hadis, terminologi hukum, dan kosakata ilmu-ilmu tertentu. Tradisi retorika (balaghah), yang mencakup ilmu-ilmu gaya, kiasan sastra, dan komposisi sastra, juga dikatalogkan dengan baik, dengan entri-entri untuk karya-karya teoritis dan praktis utama yang digunakan dalam pendidikan madrasa tingkat lanjut. Cakupan ilmu-ilmu bahasa ini mencerminkan peran mereka yang tak tergantikan sebagai instrumen melalui mana semua ilmu-ilmu Islam lainnya diakses.
content_en:
The historical literature documented in Kashf al-Zunun is extraordinarily rich, reflecting the strong Islamic tradition of historical consciousness and the centrality of biographical scholarship to the transmission of religious knowledge. Hajji Khalifa catalogs the major universal chronicles (ta'rikh 'amm) from al-Tabari's monumental history of prophets and kings through the great Mamluk and Ottoman chronicles of his own era. He also documents the genre of local histories and the annalistic chronicles kept by particular cities, dynasties, and scholarly communities. The biographical dictionary tradition (tabaqat) receives particular attention: Hajji Khalifa lists the major tabaqat works for each scholarly discipline and school, from the Tabaqat al-Kubra of Ibn Sa'd (covering the Companions and Successors) through the discipline-specific biographical dictionaries for jurists, hadith scholars, Quran reciters, grammarians, physicians, and poets of each major era.

The biographical dictionary genre (tarajim) was one of the Islamic scholarly tradition's most distinctive contributions to world literature, and Kashf al-Zunun's coverage of it is invaluable. Works like the Wafayat al-Ayan of Ibn Khallikan, the Siyar Alam al-Nubala of al-Dhahabi, and the Shajarat al-Dhahab of Ibn al-Imad al-Hanbali receive detailed entries. Hajji Khalifa notes the organizational principle of each work (chronological, alphabetical, or by scholarly generation), the author's criteria for inclusion, and the time periods covered. He also catalogs the specialized biographical dictionaries focusing on particular regions, dynasties, or scholarly communities, including the numerous biographical works devoted to Ottoman scholars that had accumulated by his time. These regional biographical works are often the primary source for scholarly genealogies and intellectual lineages outside the Arab heartlands.

The geographical literature catalogued by Hajji Khalifa spans from the practical route guides (masalik wa-mamalik) of the ninth and tenth centuries through the great encyclopedic geographies of the medieval period and into the Ottoman administrative geography of his own time. Works like al-Muqaddasi's Ahsan al-Taqasim, al-Idrisi's Kitab al-Rujari, and Yaqut al-Hamawi's Mujam al-Buldan receive substantial entries. The travel literature (rihla) genre, associated particularly with Maliki scholarship in the western Islamic world, is also well represented. Hajji Khalifa was himself a geographer of note, and his coverage of this genre reflects personal expertise: he can assess the reliability of particular geographical claims and the relationship between different works in the tradition in a way that a non-specialist bibliographer could not.

The Arabic linguistic sciences, covering grammar, lexicography, rhetoric, and prosody, are documented in Kashf al-Zunun with the thoroughness expected given their foundational importance for all other Islamic sciences. The grammarians' tabaqat and the major grammatical works from Sibawayh's Al-Kitab through the Ottoman-era madrasa grammars receive careful entries. Hajji Khalifa's coverage of the lexicographical tradition is particularly extensive, listing the major classical dictionaries (the Sihah of al-Jawhari, the Mujam of Ibn Faris, the Qamus of al-Firuzabadi and its commentary Taj al-Arus) along with specialized lexicons for hadith terms, legal terminology, and the vocabulary of particular sciences. The rhetorical tradition (balaghah), covering the sciences of style, figures of speech, and literary composition, is also well catalogued, with entries for the major theoretical and practical works used in advanced madrasa education. This coverage of the linguistic sciences reflects their indispensable role as the instruments through which all the other Islamic sciences are accessed.
content_ar: The historical literature documented in Kashf al-Zunun is extraordinarily rich, reflecting the strong Islamic التراث of historical consciousness and the centrality of biographical scholarship to the transmission of religious العلم. Hajji Khalifa catalogs the major universal chronicles (ta'rikh 'amm) from al-Tabari's monumental history of الأنبياء and kings through the great Mamluk and Ottoman chronicles of his own era. He أيضاً documents the genre of local histories and the annalistic chronicles kept by particular cities, dynasties, and العلمي communities. The biographical dictionary التراث (tabaqat) receives particular attention: Hajji Khalifa lists the major tabaqat works for each العلمي discipline and school, from the Tabaqat al-Kubra of Ibn Sa'd (covering the الصحابة and التابعين) through the discipline-specific biographical dictionaries for jurists, الحديث العلماء, القرآن الكريم reciters, grammarians, physicians, and poets of each major era. The biographical dictionary genre (tarajim) was one of the Islamic العلمي التراث's most distinctive contributions to الدنيا literature, and Kashf al-Zunun's coverage of it is invaluable. Works like the Wafayat al-Ayan of Ibn Khallikan, the Siyar Alam al-Nubala of al-Dhahabi, and the Shajarat al-Dhahab of Ibn al-Imad al-الحنبلي receive detailed entries. Hajji Khalifa notes the organizational principle of each work (chronological, alphabetical, or by العلمي generation), the author's criteria for inclusion, and the time periods covered. He أيضاً catalogs the specialized biographical dictionaries focusing on particular regions, dynasties, or العلمي communities, بما في ذلك the numerous biographical works devoted to Ottoman العلماء that had accumulated by his time. These regional biographical works are often the الأساسي source for العلمي genealogies and intellectual lineages outside the Arab heartlands. The geographical literature catalogued by Hajji Khalifa spans from the practical route guides (masalik wa-mamalik) of the تاسعاً and عاشراً centuries through the great encyclopedic geographies of the medieval period and into the Ottoman administrative geography of his own time. Works like al-Muqaddasi's Ahsan al-Taqasim, al-Idrisi's Kitab al-Rujari, and Yaqut al-Hamawi's Mujam al-Buldan receive substantial entries. The travel literature (rihla) genre, associated particularly with المالكي scholarship in the western Islamic الدنيا, is أيضاً well represented. Hajji Khalifa was himself a geographer of note, and his coverage of this genre reflects personal expertise: he can assess the reliability of particular geographical claims and the relationship between different works in the التراث in a الطريق that a non-specialist bibliographer could not. The Arabic linguistic sciences, covering grammar, lexicography, rhetoric, and prosody, are documented in Kashf al-Zunun with the thoroughness expected given their foundational importance for all other Islamic sciences.
